name: Medpac
description: A compact self-contained trauma kit — bacta gel applicator, spray coagulant, stim injectors, and a pressure bandage. Standard issue for anyone who goes where blasters get drawn. One medpac handles one casualty. After that, it's empty.

gambits.name: Field Dressing
gambits.rule: Consume this Medpac during a Treat Injury action. The patient gains [Stimmed] +1 and the result is increased by +1 Tier. One medpac per patient per rest period.

name: Breath Mask
description: A full-seal filtration mask with a twin-canister air supply and integrated goggles. Standard for anyone working in industrial zones, toxic atmospheres, or anywhere the air is trying to kill you. Uncomfortable, but less uncomfortable than suffocating on acid particulate.

traits.name: Environmental Seal
traits.description: While worn, the user suffers no penalties from toxic atmospheres, corrosive particulate, or airborne chemical agents. The integrated canister provides approximately four hours of filtered air before requiring replacement. Without a breath mask in a hostile atmosphere, all physical actions are stepped down.

name: Encrypted Comlink
description: A short-range encrypted comlink with a rolling cipher protocol. Range extends to about 50 kilometers surface-to-surface, less through heavy structure or interference. The rolling cipher is not military-grade, but it is far beyond what local law enforcement bothers to crack in the field. Allows secure coordination between characters on the same cipher key without risk of interception by standard scanning equipment.

name: Macro-binoculars
description: Military-surplus optical/electronic magnification unit. Integrates thermal imaging, low-light enhancement, and basic motion tracking. Standard kit for anyone who needs to know what they're walking into before they walk into it. The Empire makes these by the millions; half of them end up on the open market.

traits.name: Advance Reconnaissance
traits.description: Spending one full minute observing a location before entering grants the party one of the following: identify one hidden threat or guard position, remove the first-round Surprise penalty if the party initiates, or ask the GM one yes/no question about what lies ahead.

name: Glow Rod
description: An extendable baton of crystalline light-emitting material. Industrial grade, runs about eight hours on a standard power cell. Casts a wide, clean cone of white light. Essential for any dark environment — ruins, sewers, starship bilges, or anywhere the lights have been off for four thousand years.

traits.name: Illumination
traits.description: The glow rod eliminates all darkness-related step-down penalties within its cone (roughly 10 meters forward, 5 meters wide). It also makes the bearer visible — in a dark environment, anyone outside the cone can see the light source from a significant distance.

name: Security Kit
description: A compact roll of lockpicks, bypass circuits, micro-cutters, maglock shims, and fiber-optic scopes. Everything needed to open a door that does not want to be opened — mechanically or electronically. Technically restricted, since possession implies intent. In practice, every fixer and fence on the Rim carries one.

traits.name: Proper Tools
traits.description: A security kit provides the appropriate tools for bypassing locks, access panels, and electronic security systems. Without a kit, such attempts suffer a step-down penalty — improvising with a vibroblade or a bent wire is possible, but it is not elegant. The kit does not guarantee success; it guarantees you are not fighting the hardware empty-handed.

name: Syntherope
description: Fifty meters of high-tensile braided polymer rope with an integrated grappling spike and auto-spool reel. Rated for multi-occupant loads. Compact enough to fit in a utility pouch. The spike fires pneumatically and sets silently against most hard surfaces — useful when you need to rappel off the side of a citadel without announcing it.

traits.name: Vertical Mobility
traits.description: Syntherope enables climbing and rappelling that would otherwise require a powered ascender or be impossible. When used to anchor a secured line, climbing or descending a vertical surface does not require a Physique check in normal conditions — only in combat, high wind, or other active hazards.

name: Stim Pack
description: A pressurized injector loaded with combat stimulants — coagulants, adrenaline analogs, and nerve sensitizers. It does not heal injury; it convinces the body to ignore it for a while. Widely used by clone troopers, mercenaries, and anyone who cannot afford to go down in the middle of a job. One dose, one use.

gambits.name: Push Through
gambits.rule: Consume this Stim Pack as a Maneuver (no Treat Injury roll required). Clear 1 Trauma from yourself or an adjacent ally. The target gains [Stimmed] +1. Can be used mid-combat — does not require a rest.

name: Thermal Detonator
description: A palm-sized sphere of baradium behind a magnetic containment shell. Class-A military grade, deeply illegal — possession is a capital offense in many systems. Features a yield dial and programmable timer with four trigger modes: countdown, impact, proximity, or dead-man's switch. At detonation it generates superheated plasma and kinetic overpressure. The blast does not distinguish targets. One use.

traits.name: Zone Blast
traits.description:
Arm and throw as a Standard Action (zones 1–3). Roll Ranged (Reflex) vs. Evasion — the challenge is placement and timing, not armor penetration. Failure means an errant throw; the GM determines where it lands. Success: detonates at the start of your next turn.

Targets defend with Evasion — armor is irrelevant. Targets in the zone may spend their Maneuver to flee before detonation (may present Openings if Engaged). Those who remain are hit. Roll once; Effect Tier applies to all equally.

Cover applies to targets outside the blast zone only. In the same zone as the detonator, there is no cover from the source. You may throw it into your own zone — you are a target.

traits.name: Variable Yield
traits.description: The yield dial adjusts blast radius and intensity. Damage values here reflect minimum tactical deployment — the setting soldiers use to avoid killing themselves in enclosed spaces. Dialing it up is a GM narrative decision, not a combat action. It simply becomes a different kind of problem.

gambits.name: Dead Drop
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Place the detonator as a proximity mine in your zone or an adjacent unoccupied zone instead of throwing. It detonates when any figure enters or starts a turn there. Only the armer can deactivate it on their turn — no tool substitutes for the biometric key.

gambits.name: Magnetic Latch
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Attach the detonator to a specific vehicle, door, or structure in an adjacent zone (must be able to touch it). When it detonates, that target takes one additional Effect Tier step up.

gambits.name: Impact Detonation
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Set to impact mode — detonates on landing rather than your next turn. Targets have no Maneuver to flee. They defend with Evasion, but the window to exit is gone.

name: Datapad (Slicer Config)
description: A standard-format datapad with a custom hardware skin: expanded memory, a co-processor for cryptographic work, hardwired slicer port array, and a stripped-down OS with no Imperial telemetry. It looks like an ordinary datapad — the kind every merchant and bureaucrat carries — but it can interface with Imperial systems, decrypt moderate-grade ciphers, and store data that officially does not exist.

traits.name: System Interface
traits.description: The slicer datapad provides hardware and software tools for accessing electronic systems — terminals, door controls, comm arrays, Imperial databases. Without an appropriate interface device, slicing attempts are stepped down or impossible depending on the system. This datapad does not guarantee access; it ensures the attempt is technically viable.

name: Smoke Grenade
description: A pressurized canister that detonates on impact, releasing a dense chemical smoke cloud. Standard kit for covering retreats, masking advances, and creating confusion in open engagements. Non-lethal by design — the danger is what moves through the smoke, not the smoke itself.
customEffectRows: Cloud fills the zone. All attacks into, out of, or through the zone have their Control Die Stepped Down. Equally penalizing — friend and foe. Cover applies as normal.
As Fleeting. Choose 2 allies (yourself included) who can ignore vision impairment from their current position — the placement was precise enough to give two members clear lines.
As Fleeting. Any number of allies may immediately move 1 zone as a free reaction — the smoke opened the perfect moment and they take it.

traits.name: Obscuring Cloud
traits.description: The smoke cloud persists for the entire scene or until dispersed by strong wind. It does not move with the thrower. Thermal optics and Force-sensitive awareness are not affected.

gambits.name: Rolling Screen
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Allies who move through the smoke zone this round do not present Openings from movement while inside it.

gambits.name: Hot Zone
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Declare the canister incendiary before throwing. All targets who remain in the zone at the start of their next turn gain [Hazard] in addition to the smoke effects.

name: Ion Grenade
description: A compact sphere that discharges a focused electromagnetic pulse on detonation. Standard counter-droid ordnance — shuts down electronics, scrambles processors, and fries cybernetic implants. Technically non-lethal against organics, though the arcing discharge is deeply unpleasant.

traits.name: Ion Discharge
traits.description: Against droids and characters with cybernetic implants: full Effect Tier applies and stun/shutdown conditions apply normally. Against organic characters without cybernetics: reduce the Effect Tier by 1 before damage resolves. Stun conditions never apply to fully organic targets — the ion charge passes through without locking a nervous system it cannot interface with.

traits.name: Electronic Disruption
traits.description: At Legendary tier, electronic systems in the zone (doors, turrets, comms arrays) are shut down until manually reset, regardless of whether the targets were droids or organics.

gambits.name: Feedback Loop
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. A droid or cybernetic target hit must pass a Risk 4 Tech check or lose their next Standard Action as their systems reboot.

gambits.name: System Cascade
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. One droid or heavily cybernetic target in the zone risks full Shut Down — they are [Incapacitated] until an ally spends a Standard Action to reboot them, or they self-reboot at scene end.

name: Frag Grenade
description: Serrated steel body packed with Tibanna-pressurized explosive compound. Detonates into a storm of supersonic metal fragments. Standard military ordnance — cheap, reliable, effective. The Imperial Army issues them by the crate. A lot of those crates end up on the open market.

gambits.name: Clear Out
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. The arc and audible arming click telegraph what is coming. All targets in the zone must use their Maneuver to move or take cover. Once thrown, it is no longer yours to control. Those who stay are still hit when it goes.

gambits.name: Magnetic Attachment
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Clamp it to a vehicle, door, or surface (must be adjacent, must touch). It adheres — the target cannot simply step away. If attached to a moving vehicle, it goes where the vehicle goes.

gambits.name: Impact Detonation
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Set to impact mode — detonates on landing. No time to react, no Maneuver to flee. Targets who take only Fleeting damage are also knocked Prone — they dove for it, or the concussion put them down.

name: Disguise Kit
description: Pigment compounds, latex appliances, synthetic hair, contact lenses, a compact sonic sculpting tool. The kit used by actors, intelligence operatives, and people who need to walk through a checkpoint as someone else. It takes time and skill — the kit ensures the attempt is technically viable.

traits.name: Proper Disguise
traits.description: Without appropriate materials, attempting to pass as someone other than yourself is not possible, or heavily penalized at the GM's discretion. This kit removes that gate — with it, a disguised Deception attempt is viable without material penalty. Whether it succeeds is still on the character. Each significant use expends one charge; a standard kit holds approximately five.

traits.name: Species Scope
traits.description: The kit is optimized for near-human species — humans, Mirialans, Zabrak, Twi'lek, and similar builds. Disguising as a non-humanoid requires additional specialized materials not included in this kit.

name: Scanner Jammer
description: A palm-sized active jamming device that broadcasts a carefully tuned interference field. Scrambles Imperial sensor profiles within a two-zone radius. Deeply illegal — the Empire treats possession as evidence of hostile intent.

traits.name: Active Jamming
traits.description: While the jammer is running, all sensor sweeps within 2 zones must beat a Static Risk 3 higher than normal to detect its carriers. The device itself emits a detectable electromagnetic anomaly — a dedicated sensor tech can identify the interference signature as artificial on a successful check. Activating the jammer requires a Standard Action.

traits.name: Power Draw
traits.description: Runs approximately four hours on a standard power cell. No indicator — it stops without warning when the cell is depleted.

gambits.name: Ghost Signature
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Spoof a specific transponder code on one vessel or large object — it reads as authorized traffic to passive Imperial scans for the scene.

gambits.name: Directed Burn
gambits.rule: This gambit does not reduce the Effect Tier. Instead, pay the following costs: the power cell is exhausted (consumed); you consume your Standard Action to configure and fire the burst. Effect: a targeted electromagnetic pulse against one electronic lock, mag-clamp, or security beam within Engaged range. It disconnects immediately — no roll required. The jammer is dead after this.

name: Datapad
description: A standard civilian-format touchscreen data terminal — the kind sold in every market stall from Coruscant to the Outer Rim. Pre-loaded with basic star charts, a translation matrix for common languages, and a universal connector port. Useful for keeping records, referencing information, and interfacing with standard terminals.

traits.name: Data Repository
traits.description: A character who researches a subject before entering a scene may ask the GM one yes/no factual question per researched subject per scene. This represents stored reference data, not real-time intelligence.

traits.name: Universal Translator
traits.description: Translates common galactic languages in real time. Rare, extinct, or deliberately obscure languages require a Linguistics roll or a specialized language pack.

traits.name: Interface Port
traits.description: A universal connector compatible with most civilian and Imperial standard terminals. Provides a hardware link where one is needed — does not bypass security, just provides the physical connection.

name: Slicer Spike
description: A hardwired hardware bypass tool — a thumb-sized chip that physically overrides a terminal's software authentication layer when inserted. Bypasses most standard Imperial credential gates cold, without a password. Single use — the security keys burn on extraction. If the system is monitored, it flags the insertion immediately.

traits.name: Hardware Bypass
traits.description: Inserting the spike bypasses the software authentication layer entirely. Characters without the Tech discipline suffer a Static Risk 2 penalty navigating the open system — the spike opens the door but the inside still requires skill.

traits.name: Single Use
traits.description: Once used, the spike's keys are burned and the chip is spent. If the target system has active monitoring, the insertion is immediately flagged — a security alert is logged, whether or not anyone responds.

gambits.name: Combat Splice
gambits.rule: This gambit does not reduce the Effect Tier. Instead, pay the following costs: this spike is exhausted (consumed); you consume your Standard Action this round in addition to the Maneuver. Effect: perform a full terminal slicing action at Maneuver speed — in a single round, no extended time required. The spike burns, but the terminal is open.

name: Bacta Patch
description: A single-use adhesive bacta delivery pad — a thin, flexible strip saturated with a low-concentration bacta solution and a coagulating agent. Not a field surgery kit. Not a substitute for a medpac. A patch stops bleeding. That is the job. It does that job instantly, without a roll, and without taking your eyes off the firefight.

traits.name: Rapid Application
traits.description: Applying a bacta patch requires only a Maneuver — it can be used on yourself or an adjacent ally without consuming your Standard Action. No Medicine roll required.

traits.name: Clears Bleeding
traits.description: A bacta patch immediately clears the [Bleeding] condition. It does not clear Trauma, Lingering conditions, or anything else. It stops the bleeding. Nothing more.

traits.name: No Stimmed Interaction
traits.description: The bacta concentration is too low to interact with stim chemistry. Using a bacta patch never applies or modifies [Stimmed X].

name: Field Rations
description: Compressed nutrient blocks and water purification tabs. Military surplus, scavenger market staple, and what keeps most of the Outer Rim alive between ports. Doesn't taste like anything that was ever alive. Does the job. Pack of 5.

traits.name: Sustenance
traits.description: One pack provides 24 hours of food and water for one person. In survival scenarios where rations run out, all physical actions are Stepped Down after 48 hours without food or water. A pack negates this entirely for its duration.

name: Comlink
description: A palm-sized voice transceiver. No encryption, no bells, no whistles. Say a word, someone on the same frequency hears it — and so does anyone else who's listening.

traits.name: Short Range Broadcast
traits.description: Allows voice communication with any other comlink user on the same open frequency within 50 km surface-to-surface. Range reduces through heavy durasteel or active jamming.

traits.name: Open Channel
traits.description: Transmissions are unencrypted. Standard Imperial and law enforcement scanning equipment can intercept them without difficulty. For encrypted comms, see Encrypted Comlink.

name: Fusion Cutter
description: A handheld plasma cutting tool used by miners, mechanics, and anyone who has ever needed to be somewhere a door said they couldn't. Cuts through civilian-grade durasteel given time. Military blast doors take longer — but given enough power, it gets there.

traits.name: Plasma Cutter
traits.description: Can cut through any non-military-grade material given sufficient time — GM determines duration based on material thickness and composition. Military-grade durasteel and blast doors require extended time across multiple scenes unless Overload Mode is used.

traits.name: Overload Mode
traits.description: The power output dial can be set beyond safe operating limits. Doing so completes any cut as a single Standard Action regardless of material grade. The power cell burns to zero immediately — the cutter is inoperable until the cell is replaced or recharged. This is not a roll. It is a choice.

name: Binders
description: Adjustable magnetic restraint cuffs. Standard kit for bounty hunters, Imperial Stormtroopers, and law enforcement. Fits most humanoid wrist or ankle configurations. Magnetic lock — no key, dedicated release tool required.

traits.name: Magnetic Lock
traits.description: A restrained target cannot use their hands for any action requiring grip or fine motor control. Breaking free requires an Athletics (Physique) action with the Control Die stepped down 4 times. If the Control roll succeeds, the lock fails. A release tool (Security Kit) removes the step-downs entirely.

traits.name: Adjustable Fit
traits.description: Fits most humanoid species at wrist or ankle. The GM may rule non-standard anatomy — tentacles, claws, no discernible wrists — incompatible.

name: Emergency Beacon
description: A palm-sized distress transmitter that broadcasts on all standard emergency frequencies. The signal cuts through most atmospheric interference and reaches orbital sensors on any ship actively scanning. It also broadcasts to everyone else scanning the same frequency.

traits.name: Distress Broadcast
traits.description: Activation broadcasts a continuous locator signal on all standard emergency frequencies. Any vessel with active sensors within the system can detect it. Signal persists until the beacon is deactivated or destroyed.

traits.name: No Discrimination
traits.description: The beacon cannot distinguish rescuer from threat. Anyone scanning for distress signals finds it equally. A rescue is not guaranteed — only a response.

name: Aquata Breather
description: A compact rebreather that fits in the mouth. Provides roughly twenty minutes of filtered, breathable air drawn from surrounding water or thin-atmosphere environments. Jedi carry them. Smart pilots carry them. Anyone who operates near water on an unfamiliar world should carry them.

traits.name: Submersible Breath
traits.description: Provides up to 20 minutes of breathable air underwater or in thin atmosphere. Without a breather, a character begins drowning after a number of rounds equal to their Physique die value.

traits.name: Single Use Duration
traits.description: The 20-minute supply is continuous from first use. There is no partial recharge — once it runs dry, it must be resupplied or replaced.

name: Stimulant Tabs
description: Mild oral stimulants — the kind sold openly at any outpost pharmacy or cantina counter. Keep you sharp through a third watch shift, a long hyperspace run, or the hours after adrenaline crashes. Not a stim pack. Pack of 6.

traits.name: Sustained Alertness
traits.description: One tab negates the mechanical penalties from fatigue or lack of sleep for 8 hours. When the effect ends, the crash arrives — double fatigue penalties until the character completes a Long Rest.

traits.name: Not Medical Grade
traits.description: Stimulant tabs do not interact with the [Stimmed X] condition. They do not clear Trauma, remove conditions, or count as a Medicine action. They are a dietary supplement.

name: Field Toolkit
description: A hardcase kit of standard mechanical and electrical tools — hydrospanners in multiple sizes, a fusion welder, power drivers, a diagnostic reader, and assorted connectors. What a mechanic reaches for without thinking. What an amateur improvises around and still fails.

traits.name: Technical Permission
traits.description: Without a field toolkit or equivalent, Tech and Mechanics actions on vehicles, droids, starship systems, or complex electronics are Stepped Down. Some repairs may be impossible without proper tools regardless of skill.

traits.name: No Substitute
traits.description: Improvised tools impose a Step Down on Tech actions regardless of the character's skill rating. The field toolkit removes this penalty entirely.

name: Thermal Cloak
description: A full-length hooded cloak woven with thermal regulation filaments and a sealed inner lining. Works in both directions — keeps heat in on frozen worlds, reflects radiant heat on scorched ones. Standard kit on any world where the temperature is trying to kill you.

traits.name: Climate Regulation
traits.description: While worn, the character suffers no mechanical penalties from extreme heat or extreme cold environments. Truly lethal conditions — hard vacuum, volcano interiors — are outside its range. It handles survivable hostile climates, not impossible ones.

traits.name: Thermal Signature
traits.description: The active regulation filaments generate a mild infrared signature. Thermal scanning equipment or heat-sensor-equipped droids may detect the wearer more easily than an unequipped target.

name: Utility Belt
description: A wide, reinforced belt with dedicated pouches, magnetic clip attachments, and quick-release buckles. Standard kit for Jedi, Mandalorians, bounty hunters, and anyone who gets into enough trouble to need their gear right now. Everything on the belt is within reach in a single motion.

traits.name: Expanded Carry
traits.description: The utility belt allows the wearer to carry up to 4 additional small or medium gear items without encumbrance penalty. GMs may apply narrative friction — noise, bulk, reduced agility — when characters carry excessive gear without one.

traits.name: Quick Draw
traits.description: Any gear item carried on the utility belt can be retrieved as part of the Maneuver or Action used to deploy it — not as a separate step. This applies to equipment and tools only. Holstered weapons follow standard weapon draw rules.

name: Hydrospanner
description: A magnetized multi-head torque driver with variable bit attachments. The tool every shipboard mechanic reaches for first and puts down last. If something is bolted, welded, or pressure-fitted, a hydrospanner gets it loose. If you don't own one, you're not a mechanic — you're someone who watches mechanics work.

name: Wrist Chronometer
description: A rugged wrist-mounted timekeeping unit synced to galactic standard. Counts local time, hyperspace transit, and countdown timers. Nobody on the frontier asks "what time is it?" — they ask "how long do we have?" This answers that question.

name: Recording Rod
description: A stylus-sized audio/visual capture device. Records up to a hundred hours of holo-quality footage and plays it back on any standard display. Journalists carry them. So do blackmailers. The difference is editorial discretion.

name: Mem-Stik
description: A thumb-sized solid-state data slug compatible with any standard port. Copy files off a terminal, carry stolen schematics in your boot, or hand off intelligence to a contact without saying a word. The data itself is unencrypted — security is your problem.

name: Gear Bag
description: A reinforced canvas duffel with internal compartments, magnetic closures, and a shoulder strap rated for rough handling. Holds roughly twice what your pockets do. Not glamorous. Not tactical. Just a bag that doesn't fall apart when you throw it off a loading ramp.

name: Power Prybar
description: A half-meter prying tool with a powered hydraulic assist built into the grip. Pops open cargo containers, jams doors, or levers deck plates off their rails. No finesse involved — this is the tool you reach for when subtlety has already failed.

name: Rock Boots
description: Reinforced, thick-soled boots designed for volcanic badlands, scrapyard debris, and industrial decking that's been neglected for a decade. Heat-resistant soles, ankle stabilizers, and magnetic heel plates for low-gravity work. Not fast. Not quiet. But your feet stay attached.

name: Droid Caller
description: A palm-sized transmitter that broadcasts a summons directly into a bonded droid's motivational processor. Range is limited — a few hundred meters in open air, less through heavy walls. The droid has no choice in the matter. It comes. Whether it's happy about it is a different conversation.

name: Scomp Spiker
description: A crude bypass plug cobbled from a scomp link adapter and a signal scrambler. Plug it into a standard terminal port and it brute-forces the handshake protocol — the digital equivalent of kicking a door in. Single use. Burns out after one connection. Outlaw techs sell them in bundles.

traits.name: Brute Handshake
traits.description: When plugged into a standard civilian terminal, the spiker bypasses basic access locks without a Skulduggery roll. Military, Imperial, or hardened systems still require a proper slice. The spiker is consumed on use.

name: Tox Detector
description: A flat metallic sticker patch with a chemical-reactive circle in the center. Slap it on a surface, press it into food or drink, or hold it near a vent. The circle changes color if it detects common toxins, nerve agents, or biological contaminants. Doesn't tell you what the poison is — just that it's there. Pack of three.

traits.name: Color Shift
traits.description: Detects the presence of common toxins and airborne contaminants without a Medicine roll. Rare, engineered, or exotic poisons may not register. Consumed on use.

name: Vitapill
description: A fat white capsule in a foil blister. Pharmacy-grade stimulant, one step above caf and one step below anything that requires a prescription. Clears the fog after a hard hit and gets you back on your feet — for a while. Pack of four.

traits.name: Quick Recovery
traits.description: Swallowing a vitapill as a Maneuver removes one [Dazed] condition. Does not interact with [Stimmed X] and cannot clear Trauma. One pill per scene.

name: Gyro-Grappler
description: A weighted grappling head with built-in microthrusters that fire on release, correcting trajectory mid-flight. Point it at a ledge, a pipe, or a landing strut and throw — the gyro does the rest. Doesn't make you a good climber. Just gets the hook where it needs to go.

traits.name: Guided Throw
traits.description: When throwing a grappling line to anchor on a surface within 2 zones, the gyro-grappler removes any step-down penalties from distance or awkward angles. The throw is not a roll — it simply lands. Climbing the line still follows normal rules.

name: Motion Sensor Array
description: A disc-shaped passive sensor unit that clips to a belt or mounts to a wall. Detects movement within three zones and feeds a proximity readout to a linked datapad or helmet display. Doesn't tell you what moved. Just that something did.

traits.name: Proximity Alert
traits.description: While active, the motion sensor alerts the user to movement within 3 zones, even through walls and floors. This prevents surprise from approaching threats in that radius. Dense materials (heavy blast doors, underground rock) reduce effective range at GM discretion.

name: Bacta Geltab
description: A pre-dosed adhesive bacta strip in a sterile wrapper. Peel, press, and the bacta seeps into the wound on contact. Not as thorough as a medpac application, but faster and requires zero medical training. Pack of six.

traits.name: Supplemental Treatment
traits.description: Applying a geltab to a wound before or during a Treat Injury action grants a step-up to the Medicine roll. One geltab per treatment. Does not function as a standalone treatment — it supplements, not replaces.

name: Spray Plasto-Cast
description: A pressurized canister of quick-setting polymer foam. Point it at a broken limb, spray, and the cast sets rigid in seconds. Immobilizes the injury site and prevents further damage. Proper field medicine it is not — but it keeps the bone from grinding until you reach someone who knows what they're doing.

traits.name: Emergency Splint
traits.description: Applying a plasto-cast to a limb injury as a Maneuver prevents the injury from worsening due to physical activity. The character still suffers the original wound penalties, but cannot aggravate the injury further until a proper Treat Injury action is performed. Single use.

name: Shipsuit
description: Multi-pocketed fireproof coveralls made from electrically nonconductive weave. Standard uniform for working freighter crews across the Outer Rim. Protects against casual sparks, minor electrical shorts, and the kind of engine-room flash fires that kill people who wear cotton. Comes in grey. Always grey.

name: Pocket Secretary
description: A comlink-sized audio receptor paired to an encoding datapad. Activate it with a code phrase and it records, transcribes, and indexes everything it hears until you tell it to stop. Fixers use them to log meetings. Spies use them to log everything else. The playback is admissible in most courts — which is exactly why some people carry them and exactly why others check for them.

name: Spacer's Chest
description: A rectangular durasteel lockbox — one meter long, handles on every face, and sealed against vacuum. Electronic combination lock. Every spacer owns one. It sits in your quarters, under your bunk, or mag-locked to a bulkhead. This is where you keep the things that don't leave the ship: backup credits, documents, a change of clothes, and whatever else you'd rather not explain to customs.

traits.name: Combo Lock
traits.description: The chest's electronic lock requires a Moderate Skulduggery roll to pick. The chest itself is vacuum-sealed and has a body strength sufficient to survive decompression, fire, and most blunt impacts. Cutting through it requires a fusion cutter and time.

name: Coolth Backpack
description: A powered cooling unit worn as a backpack with an attached sunshield cloak that doubles as a blanket. Standard equipment on Jakku, Tatooine, and anywhere the daytime surface temperature makes unprotected exposure a medical event. The cooling unit runs eight hours on a standard cell. After that, you're just carrying a heavy bag in the heat.

traits.name: Climate Control
traits.description: While worn and powered, the coolth backpack negates all environmental penalties from extreme heat. The character does not suffer fatigue, dehydration effects, or step-down penalties from hot environments. Does not protect against direct fire or energy damage — only ambient temperature.

name: Trauma Kit
description: A heavy-duty trauma medpac built for the kind of injuries a standard medpac can't handle — deep burns, severed tissue, compound fractures, and the aftermath of explosive decompression. Bulkier than a medpac, twice the weight, and worth every gram when someone is bleeding out on the deck.

traits.name: Critical Stabilization
traits.description: When used during a Treat Injury action on a patient suffering a Critical Injury, the Trauma Kit grants a step-up to the Medicine roll and allows the medic to stabilize injuries that would otherwise require a medical facility. One kit per patient per scene. Consumed on use.

name: PTP Link
description: A point-to-point personal communicator that establishes a dedicated two-party channel — no shared frequency, no broadcast. Twenty-five kilometer range surface-to-surface. The transmissions aren't military-grade encrypted, but they're not on open comlink bands either. For a pair of operators who need to talk without the whole cantina listening, this is the minimum standard.

traits.name: Dedicated Channel
traits.description: Communications over a PTP link cannot be intercepted by standard comlink scanning equipment. Military-grade signal intelligence or dedicated slicing can still intercept, but casual eavesdropping and Imperial frequency sweeps will miss it.

name: Headstrap Macrobinoculars
description: Head-mounted magnification optics with flip-down lenses and an adjustable strap. Everything the standard macrobinoculars do, but hands-free. Scouts, snipers, and swoop gang lookouts prefer them. The downside: you look ridiculous. The upside: you see everything before everyone else does.

traits.name: Hands-Free Optics
traits.description: Provides the same reconnaissance capability as standard macrobinoculars without occupying the user's hands. Can be flipped up or down as part of any other action.

traits.name: Spotter
traits.description: When used to observe a target at Long range or beyond, the spotter can communicate target position to an ally via comlink — granting that ally a step-up on their next Ranged attack against the observed target. Requires a Maneuver to spot.

name: Magna Lock
description: A portable magnetic locking clamp that bonds to any ferrous surface — doors, hatches, cargo containers, maintenance panels. Activate it and the electromagnetic seal engages. No key. Deactivation requires the owner's code or someone with a security kit and patience. Bounty hunters use them to seal escape routes. Smugglers use them to keep customs out of specific compartments.

traits.name: Electromagnetic Seal
traits.description: Once activated on a door or container, the magna lock adds significant resistance to forced entry. Breaking through requires a dedicated effort (fusion cutter, explosives, or sustained force). Bypassing the electronic lock requires a Skulduggery roll with the Control Die stepped down once beyond the surface's normal difficulty.

name: Starship Tool Kit
description: The full kit — hydrospanners, circuit testers, plasma soldering iron, hull patch compound, diagnostic cables, and a folding work light. Everything you need to keep a freighter flying between proper maintenance stops. Without one, shipboard repairs are guesswork and prayer. With one, they're just difficult.

traits.name: Proper Equipment
traits.description: When performing starship repair actions (hull patching, system restoration, hyperdrive maintenance), the tool kit removes any step-down penalties from improvised tools. Without a starship tool kit, all shipboard repair rolls are stepped down. The kit does not grant a bonus — it removes the penalty for not having one.

name: Replar Splint
description: A rigid articulated splint with integrated micro-repulsors that immobilize a fracture site while maintaining partial joint mobility. Better than a plasto-cast by a wide margin — the patient can still move, still function, still fight if they have to. The splint holds the bone in alignment until proper surgical repair is available.

traits.name: Precision Immobilization
traits.description: Applying a replar splint during a Treat Injury action on a limb injury removes the step-down penalty that injury imposes on physical actions. The injury is not healed — it is stabilized. Full recovery still requires medical facility treatment. One splint per injury.

name: Jumper Jet Pack
description: A compact dorsal-mounted jet pack with eight fuel bursts. Each burst carries a single passenger across roughly six zones horizontally or four zones vertically. Not a flight system — a series of controlled jumps. Gets you onto a rooftop, across a chasm, or off a collapsing platform. After eight bursts, it's deadweight until you refuel.

traits.name: Burst Jump
traits.description: As a Maneuver, the wearer makes a Piloting (Reflex) check to execute a powered jump of up to 6 zones horizontally or 4 zones vertically. This movement follows the same rules as a Move Maneuver — leaving a zone where you are Engaged presents an Opening. On a failed check, the wearer lands in an adjacent zone of the GM's choice instead of the intended destination. Eight bursts per fuel load. Refueling requires a fuel canister and a Standard Action.

traits.name: Arrest Fall
traits.description: When the wearer begins falling, they may spend a burst to negate falling damage, landing in the nearest zone with a stable surface. This costs the wearer their next Maneuver — the thrust correction is violent and disorienting.

name: Holo-Journal
description: A palm-sized holographic journal with encrypted storage. Records thoughts, sketches, and short holo-clips in a personal archive. Force-sensitives use them to document visions, psychometric impressions, and meditative insights before the details fade. The encryption is basic — enough to keep casual snoops out, not enough to stop a real slicer.

name: Ryll Spice
description: Raw ryll — a coarse amber powder processed from fungal deposits on Ryloth. Cheap, abundant, and the universal currency of the Outer Rim underworld. A pouch of ryll opens doors in cantinas, pays off docking bay attendants, and greases the kind of conversations that credits alone won't start. Medicinal in small doses. Recreational in larger ones. Addictive either way.

traits.name: Underworld Currency
traits.description: Ryll is accepted as payment or barter in most underworld dealings where hard credits might draw suspicion. Offering ryll during social encounters with criminal contacts, addicts, or corrupt officials may reduce resistance or unlock cooperation at GM discretion.

name: Death Sticks
description: Slim translucent tubes filled with processed Ixetal Cilona extract. Snap the seal, inhale, and the galaxy gets warm and soft for about twenty minutes. Wildly addictive and genuinely toxic — repeated use deteriorates cognitive function permanently. Nobody with anything to lose touches them. But in the lowest cantinas on the lowest levels, a handful of death sticks buys you conversation with people who won't talk to anyone else.

traits.name: Bottom Rung Access
traits.description: Offering death sticks during social encounters with low-level criminals, addicts, street contacts, or desperate informants may open conversations that credits or reputation cannot. The user must actually share or distribute the product — merely possessing it is not sufficient.

name: Customs Holoseal
description: A forged Imperial Customs holographic seal — a thin adhesive disc that projects an authentic-looking clearance hologram when scanned. Slap one on a cargo container and it reads as pre-inspected and approved for transit. The forgery is good enough to pass routine dock scans. A dedicated customs officer running a manual verification will catch it, but most port authorities don't bother.

traits.name: Forged Clearance
traits.description: When applied to a cargo container or shipping crate, the holoseal bypasses routine automated customs scans without a roll. Manual inspection by a customs officer or Imperial agent still requires a Deception or Con check to maintain the cover story. Single use — the seal is consumed on application.

name: Sleight Box
description: A smuggler's shipping container rigged with internal repulsor baffles and mass-distribution plates. Load contraband inside and the box reads as a different weight on cargo scales — lighter, heavier, or distributed to mimic legitimate goods. Won't fool a physical search, but it defeats the automated mass-scanners that most ports rely on for flagging suspicious cargo.

traits.name: Mass Falsification
traits.description: Cargo stored in a Sleight Box registers as a different weight and mass distribution on automated cargo scanners. This defeats weight-based contraband flagging without a roll. Physical searches, chemical sniffers, or sensor-shielded scanners are unaffected. Holds up to 100 kilograms of actual cargo.

name: Sensor-Shielded Storage Compartment
description: A hidden compartment lined with sensor-baffling material — the same tech the Hutts use in their sail barges and the Empire uses in intelligence courier ships. Install it in a vessel's hull and its contents become invisible to standard sensor sweeps. The personal version fits in a cargo hold; the ship-scale installation requires a technician and a dry dock.

traits.name: Sensor Baffle
traits.description: Contents stored inside are invisible to standard sensor sweeps and cargo scanners. Military-grade deep-penetration scans may detect the shielding itself (not the contents) at GM discretion. Personal version holds a single crate worth of goods. Ship-scale installation (8,000cr) conceals an entire hidden cargo bay.

name: Surveillance Pod
description: A fist-sized autonomous sensor unit with audio pickup, visual recording, and short-range burst transmission. Plant it in a room, a corridor, or mag-clamp it to the underside of a speeder. It records continuously and transmits compressed data packets to a paired receiver at timed intervals. Military-grade optics in a civilian-looking housing. The kind of gear that intelligence agencies deny owning.

traits.name: Autonomous Surveillance
traits.description: Once placed, the pod records audio and visual data continuously for up to 72 hours. Data is burst-transmitted to a paired receiver every 15 minutes (range: 1 kilometer, or city-wide with HoloNet relay). The pod operates autonomously — no active monitoring required. Detection requires a deliberate search of the area.

name: "EAR" Eavesdropping Unit
description: A thumbnail-sized adhesive audio transmitter paired with a compact wrist-mounted receiver. Stick the bug under a table, behind a light panel, or inside a comlink casing. Everything said within five meters is transmitted live to the receiver up to 500 meters away. Imperial Intelligence calls it the "Extended Audio Receiver." Everyone else just calls it an ear.

traits.name: Audio Bug
traits.description: The transmitter picks up all conversation within 5 meters and relays it live to the paired wrist receiver (range: 500 meters, line of sight not required). Lasts 48 hours on internal power. Detection requires a deliberate sweep of the area or a scanner capable of picking up micro-frequency transmissions.

name: Comm Scanner
description: A handheld frequency-sweeping receiver that intercepts unencrypted comlink transmissions within a two-kilometer radius. Tune it to a frequency and listen in. Cycle through bands and hear everything — port authority chatter, security patrol check-ins, smuggler coordination channels. Encrypted Imperial military channels are beyond it, but most civilian and commercial traffic is wide open.

traits.name: Signal Intercept
traits.description: Intercepts unencrypted comlink transmissions within a 2-kilometer radius. The user can lock onto a specific frequency or sweep across bands. Encrypted channels (military, Imperial command, high-security corporate) cannot be intercepted. Identifying a specific transmission among heavy traffic may require an Assess action at GM discretion.

name: Subspace Tracking Beacon
description: A palm-sized magnetic transponder designed to attach to a ship's hull. Once activated, the beacon broadcasts a subspace ping at regular intervals — trackable across star systems by anyone with the paired receiver frequency. Bounty hunters plant them on targets' ships. Crime lords plant them on their own ships in case of theft. The Empire plants them on everything.

traits.name: Hyperspace Tracker
traits.description: When magnetically attached to a ship's hull, broadcasts a subspace tracking signal at timed intervals. The signal is trackable across systems using the paired receiver. Operates for approximately one standard month on internal power. Detection requires a thorough external hull inspection or a dedicated counter-surveillance sweep of the ship's sensor profile.

name: Crab Tracer
description: A self-guided micro-tracker the size of a credit chit. Activate it near a target — person, vehicle, or container — and six articulated legs carry it to the nearest surface and clamp on. It transmits location data via HoloNet burst packets to a designated receiver address. Nearly impossible to find without knowing exactly what you're looking for. The legs retract flush against the body once attached.

traits.name: Self-Guiding Tracker
traits.description: Once activated within short range of a target, the tracer autonomously attaches itself to the nearest viable surface on the designated target. Transmits location data via HoloNet burst to a paired receiver — range is effectively unlimited as long as HoloNet access exists. Internal power lasts approximately two standard weeks. Detection requires a focused counter-surveillance sweep.

name: Slave Collar
description: A heavy durasteel collar fitted with an integrated electroshock mechanism and a paired handheld director unit. The collar delivers incapacitating shocks on command — or automatically if the wearer moves beyond a preset range from the director. Standard equipment in Zygerrian slaving operations and Hutt labor camps. Possession alone is a capital offense in Republic-legacy systems. Out here, nobody asks.

traits.name: Compliance Device
traits.description: The director unit can trigger an electroshock at any time, inflicting [Stunned] on the wearer. If the wearer moves beyond the preset range (configurable, up to 100 meters), the shock triggers automatically. Removing the collar without the director unit's release code requires a Mechanics check and risks triggering a lethal-intensity discharge.

name: Fingerprint Masque
description: A pair of thin dermal gloves that bond to the wearer's fingertips at the cellular level, rewriting their fingerprint pattern to match a pre-loaded biometric profile. Apply them, wait sixty seconds for the cellular bonding to set, and every scanner in the galaxy reads you as someone else. The masque lasts approximately twelve hours before the cellular bond degrades and the wearer's natural prints reassert.

traits.name: Biometric Forgery (Fingerprint)
traits.description: While active (approximately 12 hours), the wearer's fingerprints match a pre-loaded biometric profile. All fingerprint scanners, identity terminals, and biometric checkpoints read the forged identity. Requires a valid biometric profile to load — the masque does not generate fake prints, it replicates real ones. Single use.

name: Retinal Disguiser
description: A set of micro-thin contact lenses that project a false retinal pattern over the wearer's natural one. The most expensive piece of infiltration gear on the black market for a reason — retinal scans are the gold standard of Imperial biometric security. With a valid retinal profile loaded, these lenses defeat every civilian and military-grade retinal scanner currently deployed. The lenses degrade after a single use cycle.

traits.name: Biometric Forgery (Retinal)
traits.description: While worn, the wearer's retinal pattern matches a pre-loaded biometric profile. Defeats all standard retinal scanners including Imperial military-grade checkpoints. Requires a valid retinal profile to load. Lenses degrade after approximately 8 hours or one scan cycle, whichever comes first. Single use.

name: Unitech "Patch" Interface Bridge
description: A universal cross-architecture adapter that bridges incompatible computer systems, droid architectures, and data formats. Plug one end into an Imperial terminal and the other into a Separatist-era droid brain and the Patch translates between them in real time. Designed by Unitech for corporate system integration work. Outlaw techs discovered it's equally useful for connecting things that were never meant to talk to each other.

traits.name: Universal Bridge
traits.description: Allows data transfer, slicing, and interface actions between incompatible system architectures — different manufacturers, eras, or encryption standards. Without a Patch or equivalent bridge, cross-architecture operations are impossible regardless of skill. The bridge translates in real time but does not bypass security — it simply makes the connection possible.

name: Andris White Spice
description: Crystalline white powder refined from raw Kessel spice. Andris floods the sensory cortex — colors sharpen, sounds resolve into layers, micro-expressions become obvious. Military scouts dose before recon. Sabacc sharks dose before the final table. The cost is simple: while your brain is processing everything at maximum fidelity, your body forgets how to absorb punishment.

traits.name: Heightened Processing
traits.description: After ingestion (scene duration), gain [Optimized] on Assess maneuvers made with Insight or Investigation, and on the Join Battle maneuver. While under the effect, Arena Traumas soak 1 Vitality instead of the normal 2. One dose per scene. Addictive with repeated use at GM discretion.

name: Carsunum Black Spice
description: Jet-black crystalline powder mined from the deepest caverns of Sevarcos. Carsunum doesn't sharpen one system — it overclocks everything. Reflexes, cognition, raw physical output. For a few hours, every action you take lands like you meant it. When the effect collapses, it takes pieces of you with it.

traits.name: Total Override
traits.description: After ingestion (a few hours, GM discretion), every successful action is treated as at least Masterful — the Power result resolves as 8+ minimum, granting +1 Effect tier above Fleeting baseline. Natural Masterful or higher results are unaffected. When the effect ends, gain [Weakened] on all Power die rolls for a duration matching the enhanced period. Take 1 automatic Vitality damage per hour enhanced (no soak). Force-sensitive characters lose access to Force maneuvers for the full duration. Extremely addictive.

name: Glitterstim
description: Sealed in slim black cylinders to protect the light-sensitive fibers within. Crack the seal, expose it to light, and the translucent strands glow pearlescent blue. Dissolve them on your tongue. For a brief, terrifying window, you can read the surface thoughts of anyone in the room. The most expensive spice in the galaxy. One hit and you understand why the Pyke Syndicate kills to control the supply.

traits.name: Surface Telepathy
traits.description: After ingestion (one scene), Assess actions targeting sentient characters may include questions about surface thoughts and immediate intent — a category of information normally inaccessible. The roll and information economy function as normal; Glitterstim simply opens another book to ask questions from. On failure, the telepathic noise is too dense to parse. Force-sensitive targets feel the intrusion automatically. When the effect fades, gain [Disoriented] for the remainder of the scene. Single use. Extremely addictive.

name: Peer Snooper Goggles
description: Bulky low-profile goggles with layered optical filters — low-light amplification, infrared overlay, and 4x magnification. Originally designed for Corporate Sector security teams running night operations. Fell off a lot of transports. Half the bounty hunters on the Rim wear a pair pushed up on their foreheads.

traits.name: Night Eyes
traits.description: While worn, negate any increased Risk or narrative restrictions imposed by darkness, low-light, or obscured visibility. Extends effective observation range for Assess and ranged targeting to Long range in any lighting condition. Sudden bright flashes while in low-light mode inflict [Disoriented] — Immediate — as the amplifiers overload.

name: Veridicator
description: A palm-sized biometric scanner that reads pulse, respiration, galvanic skin response, and micro-expression patterns within a five-meter arc. Point it at someone during a conversation and the readout tells you whether they believe what they're saying. Imperial Intelligence developed it for interrogation rooms. Somehow, units keep ending up in the hands of fixers, lawyers, and very paranoid crime lords.

traits.name: Truth Reader
traits.description: While active during conversation, the device's biometric readouts reinforce visual cues on an Assess (Insight) action. On success, the user immediately knows whether the target is being Deceptive or Truthful — not whether they are lying about a specific subject, but whether they are guarding their words or protective of information. If the target is actively resisting (aware of observation, trained in counter-interrogation), their Resolve reduces the Power result as normal — but the Deceptive/Truthful binary is delivered regardless of the final effect tier, as long as the Assess succeeds. Does not function on droids or species without standard biorhythms.

name: Jammer Pack
description: A heavy backpack-mounted broadband noise generator. Flip the switch and every comlink within a hundred and fifty meters goes dead — nothing but screaming static across all frequencies. Security channels, encrypted military bands, civilian calls. In that silence, nobody can call for backup.

traits.name: Comms Blackout
traits.description: When activated, all comlink transmissions within the area are blocked — no outgoing calls, no incoming signals, no distress beacons. Any NPC attempting to call reinforcements, trigger a remote alarm, or coordinate a tactical response automatically fails. Allies are equally jammed unless using hardline or physical-contact communication. Does not affect sensor sweeps. Requires the pack to be worn and powered (occupies the back slot). Single action to activate or deactivate.

name: Lock Breaking Kit
description: A hardcase the size of a datapad containing a frequency scanner, magnetic pulse emitter, and a rack of adaptive code cylinders. Designed to defeat electronic locks, mag-seals, and coded entryways. The kit reads the lock's cycling frequency, generates a matching pulse, and convinces the mechanism it just received a valid code cylinder.

traits.name: Code Breaker
traits.description: Required to attempt bypassing high-grade electronic locks, mag-seals, and coded security doors that cannot be defeated with improvised tools. Roll the Skulduggery or Security action normally. The kit's adaptive cylinders are consumed on use against the highest-grade locks (one cylinder per attempt, kit holds 3).
